tool.measured_command:
export NPROC="${NPROC:-$(nproc)}"
export PATH="$PATH:$HOME/local/bin"
export CC="sccache cc"
export CXX="sccache c++"
./x.py build -j"$NPROC" --unittest --compiler gcc -D CMAKE_C_COMPILER_LAUNCHER=sccache -D CMAKE_CXX_COMPILER_LAUNCHER=sccache --dep-dir build-deps
./x.py test cpp
./x.py test go build -parallel 2
setup_commands: export NPROC="${NPROC:-$(nproc)}"; curl -fsSLO https://download.redis.io/releases/redis-6.2.14.tar.gz; tar -xzf redis-6.2.14.tar.gz; mkdir -p "$HOME/local/bin"; (cd redis-6.2.14 && CC=cc CXX=c++ BUILD_TLS=yes make -j"$NPROC" redis-cli redis-server && mv src/redis-cli src/redis-server "$HOME/local/bin/")
tool.notes: Kvrocks is primarily C++. The public issue starts with slow Go tests, but active comments call out compilation/building and third-party C++ dependencies as a large CI cost. This proof measures the representative Ubuntu GCC build, C++ unit tests, and Go integration cases while routing C/C++ compilation through the BoringCache-backed sccache proxy. Go integration runtime is expected to remain as the honest tail.
